seoDescription: ValorForge has led broadband Fabric operations for Tier 1 carriers, national-scale telecom topology mapping on Palantir Foundry, and federal network resilience planning with NOAA.
headline: Proof in Practice
intro: We earn trust through delivery. These engagements represent the capabilities we bring to every new mission — broadband domain expertise, federal program management discipline, and the ability to operate at national scale.

caseStudies.label: Federal Broadband Programs
caseStudies.title: Broadband Fabric Operations — Tier 1 National Carrier
caseStudies.situation: A Tier 1 national carrier — one of the largest BDC filers in the country — needed to manage biannual availability data filings against the FCC's Broadband Serviceable Location Fabric while navigating systemic address matching failures, missing rural locations, and an opaque bulk challenge process.
caseStudies.role: Led the cross-functional team responsible for Fabric data quality assessment, BDC filing workflow management, and bulk challenge preparation and submission. Developed automated address matching workflows to improve match rates against Fabric location IDs. Documented and escalated systematic Fabric errors — including missing locations in newly developed areas and MDU unit count discrepancies. Managed the end-to-end challenge lifecycle from evidence gathering through submission and adjudication tracking.
caseStudies.outcome: [To be populated with quantifiable results — e.g., improved address match rate from X% to Y%, successfully challenged Z locations across N states, reduced filing preparation cycle from X weeks to Y weeks, identified $X in deployment obligation exposure from Fabric inaccuracies]
capabilities: FCC Broadband Data Collection compliance and filing management
Fabric data quality assessment and challenge process navigation
ISP stakeholder coordination and operational workflow design

caseStudies.label: Geospatial Intelligence
caseStudies.title: National-Scale Telecom Topology Mapping — Palantir Foundry
caseStudies.situation: A major telecom operator required unified geospatial visibility across its network planning, construction, operations, and resilience functions at national scale. Existing systems were siloed, with no single source of truth for network topology or infrastructure status.
caseStudies.role: Program management and delivery leadership for a Palantir Foundry implementation that created a real-time digital twin of the operator's telecom infrastructure. Coordinated cross-functional data integration from planning systems, construction management, field operations, and environmental risk sources. Managed stakeholder alignment across engineering, operations, and executive leadership.
caseStudies.outcome: [To be populated — e.g., unified visibility across X million network assets, Y% reduction in planning cycle time, Z improvement in disaster response readiness, consolidated N previously siloed data sources]
capabilities: Large-scale program management for geospatial data platforms
Multi-source data integration at national scale

caseStudies.label: Federal Agency Collaboration
caseStudies.title: Network Disaster Planning — NOAA Partnership
caseStudies.situation: A federal initiative required the integration of NOAA environmental risk data with telecom infrastructure mapping to enable proactive network disaster planning and resilience assessment.
caseStudies.role: Program leadership coordinating multi-agency data sharing between federal environmental agencies and telecom infrastructure operators. Managed the geospatial integration of weather risk models, flood zones, wildfire exposure, and hurricane track data with network topology and critical infrastructure locations.
caseStudies.outcome: [To be populated — e.g., covered X states/territories, integrated Y environmental data layers, improved disaster preparedness assessment for Z critical facilities, reduced response planning time by N%]
capabilities: Federal agency collaboration and multi-agency coordination
Geospatial data integration from diverse federal sources
Critical infrastructure protection and resilience planning
Program management in classified/sensitive data environments

footerCta.text: Ready to see what disciplined delivery looks like?
